commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=27210a664afeccd55537a4f30bad78d5ee40ce36)
commands.review_summary:
The branch deletes the existing LTS release policy RFC and adds a renamed RFC defining monthly `stable` and `daily` release lines, stable backport rules, coverage targets, and release tooling requirements.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an RFC documentation/policy PR rather than a runtime bug. The review findings are source-reproducible by comparing PR head frontmatter and path against README.md and rfcs/0000-template.md on current main.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A repair worker can fix the mechanical RFC path and frontmatter issues, but maintainers still need to decide the release-channel policy before merge.

target.title: docs: define monthly stable release policy
target.body:
## Context

- Replace the opt-in `lts` channel model with a trailing monthly `stable` line and a current-month `daily` line.
- Standardize releases on `YYYY.M.PATCH` and reserve `YYYY.M.33` as each month's first stable version.
- Limit stable backports to security and reliability fixes, including covered OpenClaw-maintained plugins.
- Add per-LTS-surface integration coverage baselines, a greater-than-90% target, prioritized issue handling, and explicit pre-release tooling and documentation requirements.
- Rename the RFC to `0001-stable-reelease-policy.md`.

## Testing

- `git diff --cached --check`
- Verified required version, rotation, coverage, backport, CLI tooling, plugin tooling, and documentation policy invariants.
- Confirmed the RFC repository has no configured documentation build or Markdown linter.
target.author: kevinlin-openai

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
